speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Now say, what would Augustus Caesar with us?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: When Julius Caesar, whose remembrance yet

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Lives in men’s eyes and will to ears and tongues

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Be theme and hearing ever, was in this Britain

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And conquered it, Cassibelan, thine uncle,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Famous in Caesar’s praises no whit less

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Than in his feats deserving it, for him

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And his succession granted Rome a tribute,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Yearly three thousand pounds, which by thee lately

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Is left untendered.

speaker: QUEEN
text: And, to kill the marvel,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Shall be so ever.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: There be many Caesars

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Ere such another Julius. Britain’s a world

speaker: CLOTEN
text: By itself, and we will nothing pay

speaker: CLOTEN
text: For wearing our own noses.

speaker: QUEEN
text: That opportunity

speaker: QUEEN
text: Which then they had to take from ’s, to resume

speaker: QUEEN
text: We have again.—Remember, sir, my liege,

speaker: QUEEN
text: The Kings your ancestors, together with

speaker: QUEEN
text: The natural bravery of your isle, which stands

speaker: QUEEN
text: As Neptune’s park, ribbed and palèd in

speaker: QUEEN
text: With rocks unscalable and roaring waters,

speaker: QUEEN
text: With sands that will not bear your enemies’ boats

speaker: QUEEN
text: But suck them up to th’ topmast. A kind of conquest

speaker: QUEEN
text: Caesar made here, but made not here his brag

speaker: QUEEN
text: Of “came, and saw, and overcame.” With shame—

speaker: QUEEN
text: The first that ever touched him—he was carried

speaker: QUEEN
text: From off our coast, twice beaten; and his shipping,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Poor ignorant baubles, on our terrible seas

speaker: QUEEN
text: Like eggshells moved upon their surges, cracked

speaker: QUEEN
text: As easily ’gainst our rocks. For joy whereof

speaker: QUEEN
text: The famed Cassibelan, who was once at point—

speaker: QUEEN
text: O, giglet Fortune!—to master Caesar’s sword,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Made Lud’s Town with rejoicing fires bright

speaker: QUEEN
text: And Britons strut with courage.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Come, there’s no more tribute to be paid. Our

speaker: CLOTEN
text: kingdom is stronger than it was at that time, and,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: as I said, there is no more such Caesars. Other of

speaker: CLOTEN
text: them may have crooked noses, but to owe such

speaker: CLOTEN
text: straight arms, none.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Son, let your mother end.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: We have yet many among us can grip as hard

speaker: CLOTEN
text: as Cassibelan. I do not say I am one, but I have a

speaker: CLOTEN
text: hand. Why tribute? Why should we pay tribute? If

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Caesar can hide the sun from us with a blanket or

speaker: CLOTEN
text: put the moon in his pocket, we will pay him tribute

speaker: CLOTEN
text: for light; else, sir, no more tribute, pray you now.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: You must know,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Till the injurious Romans did extort

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: This tribute from us, we were free. Caesar’s ambition,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Which swelled so much that it did almost stretch

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: The sides o’ th’ world, against all color here

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Did put the yoke upon ’s, which to shake off

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Becomes a warlike people, whom we reckon

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Ourselves to be. We do say, then, to Caesar,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Our ancestor was that Mulmutius which

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Ordained our laws, whose use the sword of Caesar

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Hath too much mangled, whose repair and franchise

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Shall, by the power we hold, be our good deed,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Though Rome be therefore angry. Mulmutius made our laws,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Who was the first of Britain which did put

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: His brows within a golden crown and called

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Himself a king.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I am sorry, Cymbeline,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That I am to pronounce Augustus Caesar—

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Caesar, that hath more kings his servants than

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thyself domestic officers—thine enemy.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Receive it from me, then: war and confusion

speaker: LUCIUS
text: In Caesar’s name pronounce I ’gainst thee. Look

speaker: LUCIUS
text: For fury not to be resisted. Thus defied,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I thank thee for myself.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Thou art welcome, Caius.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Thy Caesar knighted me; my youth I spent

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Much under him. Of him I gathered honor,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Which he to seek of me again perforce

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Behooves me keep at utterance. I am perfect

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: That the Pannonians and Dalmatians for

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Their liberties are now in arms, a precedent

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Which not to read would show the Britons cold.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: So Caesar shall not find them.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Let proof speak.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: His Majesty bids you welcome. Make pastime

speaker: CLOTEN
text: with us a day or two, or longer. If you seek us afterwards

speaker: CLOTEN
text: in other terms, you shall find us in our saltwater

speaker: CLOTEN
text: girdle; if you beat us out of it, it is yours. If

speaker: CLOTEN
text: you fall in the adventure, our crows shall fare the

speaker: CLOTEN
text: better for you, and there’s an end.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: So, sir.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: I know your master’s pleasure, and he mine.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: All the remain is welcome.
